title: Junk Bond Underwriting
description: Milken's crowd wants you on a high-yield deal. The fees are obscene. So is the risk. Pour the champagne and read the fine print.

title: Leveraged Buyout
description: Take a sleepy conglomerate private, load it with debt, strip it for parts. Barbarians at the gate — and you hold the keys.

title: S&L Fire Sale
description: The RTC is dumping a failed thrift's loan book. Gold nuggets bobbing in toxic sludge. Strong stomach required; weak ones need not apply.

title: Interest Rate Swap Book
description: A corporate treasurer wants to hedge. Tidy, technical, lucrative. Nobody understands it but you — which is rather the point.

title: Latin American Debt
description: Brady bonds, restructured sovereign paper, double-digit yields. The IMF's got their back. Probably. Mostly.

title: Mortgage Securitization
description: Slice a thousand home loans into bonds and sell the slices. Alchemy with a rating agency's blessing. What could go wrong?

title: Dot-Com IPO
description: No revenue, no profits, no problem. A website with a sock-puppet mascot wants to go public at a billion. The music is still playing.

title: Proprietary Trading Desk
description: Hire the gunslingers, hand them the firm's capital, let them swing. Glass-Steagall's crumbling — go make a killing. Or get killed.

title: Telecom Buildout Loan
description: Fiber in the ground, dreams in the air. Everyone's wiring the world at once. Heady stuff — and a glut you can already smell.

title: Universal Bank Merger
description: Glass-Steagall is dead. Bolt an investment bank onto your commercial one and become a colossus. Synergies! (Someone always says synergies.)
